response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with 12+ years across web, backend, and distributed systems, but the role is C++-centric and the resume shows almost no C++ depth. C++ appears only briefly (a ~6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis) and is absent from the listed skills, which center on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of message brokers, MQTT, IoT, industrial automation, or IEC 62443/SDL security practices required here. While distributed/low-latency and concurrent work is present, the core technical and domain alignment is too weak for a Senior C++ Digital Solutions role.
strengths: Deep distributed-systems and low-latency experience (high-throughput microservices, async I/O, fibers)
Strong software-craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development
Broad cross-platform background and mature ownership/communication via talks and workshops
concerns: Minimal C++/STL experience and not listed among core skills
No exposure to MQTT, message brokers, IoT, or industrial automation protocols
No evidence of secure development lifecycle or IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards

key_factors.factor: Industrial/IoT and IEC 62443 security domain fit
